message:
style,feat(server): cargo fmt pass + compose-dispatch console feedback on failures

Two small threads of change bundled because they're the only remaining
non-feature modifications across cli/core/db/server:

Pure `cargo fmt` pass
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs: `handle_doctor_pack_archive`
  reformatted — `.ok_or_else` chain unfolded, multi-line println!
  argument wrapping normalized, `fmt_bytes` match arm wrapped.
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs: test-module
  `TestEnvOverrideGuard::set(&[(…)])` calls collapsed to single-line
  form when the slice is one element.
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/queries.rs: test-module byte-slice
  array literals wrapped to fit width; one `assert!(..., "…", raw)`
  argument list collapsed.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs (the functional piece)
- `dispatch_compose_envelope` now feeds two user-visible failure
  cases into the TUI console log so the composer can see that the
  send didn't silently succeed:
    * "cannot open DB for send" — pushes "❌ Compose: could not
      open database — message not sent".
    * generic "failed to dispatch message" — pushes "❌ Compose:
      failed to send message — {e}" with the error's Display.
  Previously these only went to tracing; from the user's point of
  view the TUI appeared to accept a compose that in fact never
  landed. Uses `push_console_log` exactly like every other TUI
  console entry, so styling stays consistent.
- Module-declaration order tidied: `pub mod maintenance` moves past
  `mod mail_ui` so the file groups pub/priv modules adjacent.
- A multi-line `asupersync::runtime::spawn_blocking(move || { ... })`
  call got its indentation collapsed by rustfmt — no behaviour change.
